features.summary: Broadcom reported consolidated net revenue of $8.7 billion for Q2 FY2023, an 8% increase year-on-year. Semiconductor Solutions revenue rose 9% to $6.8 billion, while Infrastructure Software grew 3% to $1.9 billion. The company anticipates Q3 revenue of $8.85 billion, reflecting a 5% year-on-year increase, driven by strong demand in AI and networking sectors.
features.management_outlook: Management expects generative AI revenue to grow from 15% of semiconductor revenue in Q2 FY2023 to over 25% by FY2024. They anticipate Q3 semiconductor revenue growth in the mid-single digits year-on-year, with continued strength in networking and server storage connectivity. The company is also progressing on its acquisition of VMware, expecting to close the transaction in FY2023.
features.risks: Broadcom highlighted potential risks related to inventory management and demand moderation in IT infrastructure, particularly as growth rates have moderated from previous quarters. Additionally, the company noted ongoing competition in the networking and AI sectors, which could impact market share and pricing strategies.
features.key_drivers: Key growth drivers include the strong deployment of generative AI technologies, particularly in networking and server storage solutions. The company is also benefiting from strategic collaborations in wireless connectivity and continued demand from hyperscale customers. The successful integration of AI capabilities into existing product lines is expected to enhance revenue growth moving forward.
